---
title: "Untitled Presentation"
subtitle: "A Quarto Perspective"
thanks: "yourwebsite.com"

author:
  - name: Author One
    orcid: 0000-0000-0000-0000
    affiliations:
      - ref: inst1

  - name: Author Two
    orcid: 0000-0000-0000-0000
    affiliations:
      - ref: inst2

short-author: "Author One"

affiliations:
  - id: inst1
    name: University One
    department: Department of Economics

  - id: inst2
    name: University Two

date: "today"
date-format: long

fontfamily: lato

format:
  qbeamer-beamer: default
---

## Outline {#sec-outline}

This skeleton demonstrates the main features of **qbeamer**:

1. Text formatting and Markdown basics
2. Custom color palette
3. Lists and enumeration
4. Callouts (note and warning)
5. Math and equations
6. Figures and images
7. Navigation buttons
8. Footnotes

# Text and Markdown

## Text Formatting

You can use standard Quarto Markdown:

- **Bold text** with `**bold**`
- *Italic text* with `*italic*`
- `inline code` with backticks
- [Hyperlinks](https://quarto.org) with `[text](url)`

## Code Blocks

A code block with syntax highlighting:

```r
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = wt, y = mpg)) +
  geom_point() +
  theme_minimal()
```

# Custom Colors

## Color Palette

qbeamer defines four custom colors you can use with La\TeX\ commands:

- \textcolor{blue}{Blue (0, 114, 178)} --- titles, items, links
- \textcolor{red}{Red (213, 94, 0)} --- warnings, emphasis
- \textcolor{green}{Green (0, 158, 115)} --- success, highlights

Section break slides use a yellow background automatically.

Use them inline: `\textcolor{blue}{your text}` or `\color{red}{your text}`

## Colored Math

Colors work inside math environments too:

$$
\hat{\beta} = \underset{\beta}{\text{\color{red}{argmin}}} \sum_{i=1}^{n} \left( y_i - X_i \beta \right)^2
$$

The $\text{\color{blue}{OLS estimator}}$ minimizes the $\text{\color{green}{sum of squared residuals}}$.

# Lists and Enumeration

## Bullet Points

Bullet points use dashes with custom spacing:

- First level item
  - Second level item
  - Another second level
- Back to first level
- Items are automatically justified

## Numbered Lists

1. First item with automatic numbering
2. Second item
3. Third item with nested bullets:
   - Sub-item one
   - Sub-item two

# Callouts

## Note Callout

::: {.callout-note}
## Important Concept

Callout notes use the **blue** color scheme and are ideal for
highlighting key concepts or definitions.

$$
\mathbb{E}[X] = \sum_{i=1}^{n} x_i \cdot P(X = x_i)
$$
:::

## Warning Callout

::: {.callout-warning}
## Caution

Warning callouts use the **red** color scheme. Use them to flag
assumptions, limitations, or common pitfalls.
:::

# Math and Equations

## Inline and Display Math

Inline math: The sample mean is $\bar{x} = \frac{1}{n}\sum_{i=1}^n x_i$.

Display math with alignment:

$$
\begin{aligned}
\mathcal{L}(\theta | x) &= \prod_{i=1}^{n} f(x_i | \theta) \\
\ell(\theta | x) &= \sum_{i=1}^{n} \log f(x_i | \theta) \\
\hat{\theta}_{MLE} &= \underset{\theta}{\mathrm{argmax}} \; \ell(\theta | x)
\end{aligned}
$$

## Matrices

$$
X = \begin{bmatrix}
1 & x_1 \\
1 & x_2 \\
\vdots & \vdots \\
1 & x_n
\end{bmatrix}, \quad
\hat{\beta} = (X'X)^{-1}X'y
$$

# Figures

## Including Images

Use standard Quarto syntax for images:

```markdown
![Caption](path/to/figure.pdf){width=85% fig-align="center"}
```

With cross-references:

```markdown
![Caption](figure.png){#fig-label width=70%}
```

Reference in text with `@fig-label`.

# Navigation and Footnotes

## Navigation Buttons

\BLButton{\hyperlink{sec-outline}{\beamerbutton{Outline}}}
\BLButton{\hyperlink{sec-appendix}{\beamerbutton{Appendix}}}

Add navigation buttons to any slide using La\TeX\ commands:

```latex
\BLButton{\hyperlink{sec-outline}{\beamerbutton{Outline}}}
```

Buttons appear in the bottom-left of the footline.

## Footnotes

Standard Markdown footnotes work in Quarto^[This is a regular footnote.].

For the title slide, the `thanks` YAML field creates a dagger footnote automatically.

## Vertical Spacing

Use La\TeX\ spacing commands for fine control:

\vspace{0.5cm}

- `\vspace{0.5cm}` --- vertical space
- `\medskip` --- medium vertical skip
- `\smallskip` --- small vertical skip

\medskip

These are useful for adjusting layout within slides.

# Appendix {#sec-appendix}

## Resources

- **Quarto documentation**: [https://quarto.org](https://quarto.org)
- **Beamer user guide**: [https://ctan.org/pkg/beamer](https://ctan.org/pkg/beamer)
- **qbeamer source**: [https://github.com/GabboCg/qbeamer](https://github.com/GabboCg/qbeamer)
- **Inspired by**: [beamer-tips](https://github.com/paulgp/beamer-tips)
